title: Peloton (PTON) Q3 2026 Earnings Transcript
text: Peloton Q3 2026 Earnings Summary. Key Financial Metrics: Revenue & Profitability Q3 total revenue $631 million (exceeded guidance by $6M). Gross margin 51.9% (up 90 bps YoY, but 210 bps below guidance). Adjusted EBITDA $126 million (41% YoY growth). Free cash flow $151 million (59% YoY increase). Balance Sheet Strength: Cash position $1.13 billion. Net debt $173 million (70% reduction YoY). Retired $200 million of convertible debt in February. Subscription Metrics: Connected Fitness subscriptions 2.662 million. Average monthly churn 1.2% (7 bps improvement YoY). Strategic Highlights: Business Diversification The company announced a major content licensing partnership with Spotify, bringing over 1,400 Peloton classes across strength, pilates, bar, yoga, meditation, outdoor and cardio to Spotify Premium subscribers globally, creating a high-margin, diversified revenue stream. Commercial Expansion: Commercial Business Unit revenue grew 14% YoY, with new gym-focused equipment launching Q2 FY2027. Management estimates only 3% market penetration of the $10 billion commercial fitness segment. Product Innovation: Pilates modality grew 48% in Q3. New hardware launches planned for fall 2026. Focus on strength category expansion. Fiscal 2026 Guidance: Revenue $2.42-$2.44 billion (2% YoY decline). Adjusted EBITDA $470-$480 million (18% YoY growth). Free cash flow ~$350 million. Historic milestone Company expects positive net income and operating income for first time. Capital Allocation Strategy: CEO Peter Stern emphasized profound strategic optionality following debt reduction, indicating plans to evaluate debt refinancing, share repurchases, and targeted investments pending permanent CFO appointment. The company will pursue its first credit rating process to optimize borrowing costs.
